conditions.optimizer: AdamW with FP32 parameters and states
fp8_modules: dense first FFN with combined gate/up projection
shared FFNs with combined gate/up projection

recipe.name: Transformer Engine Float8CurrentScaling
recipe.format: HYBRID: E4M3 forward and E5M2 backward
recipe.note: Current Scaling has no delayed scale or amax-history state. It computes the current tensor max during each quantization.

logical_vocabulary.loss_semantics: The Liger CE kernel receives n_cols=128001 and padded dlogits are zeroed, so dummy rows do not enter the softmax or receive LM-head gradients.

torch_compile.decision: Keep eager as the default. Dynamo reached its recompile limit on mutable AttnRes state and produced no throughput benefit.

remaining_bottlenecks: The CE reduction is the largest single kernel at 9.2% of GPU time.
The two largest KDA backward kernels total 255.6ms, or 11.3% of GPU time.
The main AttnRes forward and backward kernels total 213.6ms, or 9.4% of GPU time.
Current Scaling quantization is measurable at about 7.0%, but its fresh max scan alone is only about 1.9%.

acceptance.h100: complete test suite passed with only non-H100 platform skips
acceptance.fp8_loss: Loss and gradients compared to a BF16 logical-vocabulary reference; padded rows had exactly zero gradient.
acceptance.activation_checkpointing: Full H100 model forward/backward passed with Transformer Engine checkpoint recomputation.

decision: Use configs/h100-fp8-current.json as the fastest measured one-H100 primary profile.
Keep attention, routing, routed experts, reductions, parameters, and AdamW state out of FP8.
Keep MegaBlocks for routed experts because TE 2.16 GroupedLinear regressed the full H100 step.
Keep torch.compile disabled by default because it was neutral and hit mutable-state recompilations.
